Brian Dowling and his fashion-forward design team continue their mission to prove to the nation that it is possible achieve a dream home renovation at little cost.
Jeff goes to the rescue of pot-holers in a cave and takes the opportunity to set the record straight about himself and restore his reputation.
A rotating panel comprised of women from the entertainment and journalism worlds discuss issues from a female perspective, from politics and current affairs to all the latest gossip.
Brian McFadden presents the dining challenge show. Dancer Louie Spence throws a dinner party at his home for four strangers as he plays mystery celebrity host.
Celebrity transformations from an amateur to a restaurant-level chef.
After Matron is attacked by a thief, everyone is surprised with the robber is unmasked, and the hospital must handle a TB epidemic.
Dr. Weatherill finds the root cause of a family tragedy, and Nurse Taylor's future looks well while Alun's is uncertain.
Some of the top celebrities across Ireland are interviewed while the hosts feature knowledgeable advice on topics that cover multiple topics and industries.
A look at the challenges and hard-won joys of buying a home in Ireland.
An examination of the top news and entertainment events of 1998 set to music.
An examination of the top news and entertainment events of 1999 set to music.
Paul McGrath played at three major tournaments throughout Irelands golden era. He relives a club and international career and his battles on and off the field.
Retired rugby union player Alan Quinlan speaks about his nine-year career in Ireland, during which he won 27 caps and appeared in three World Cups.

An air hostess, a law graduate and a trampolining champion hope to win a romantic date with a businessman using their stylish wits.
The pressure in the kitchen reaches boiling point as two chefs go head to head. But when service is over romance is well and truly on the menu.
Contestants this time are Kate, John Paul and Lynne.
Judge Judy presides over her courtroom with wit and wisdom.
A teenage homeowner says her 53-year-old tenant jumped her over a Wi-Fi dispute; the tenant says she was a slumlord with 22 city violations.
A mix of celebrity interviews, entertainment news, current affairs discussions, a look at sports competitions, health columns, cooking tips and tricks, and more.

Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues.
Gordon and Frankie end up in a dangerous predicament when they aid an injured trawler-man on board a sinking boat, and Matron resigns to start a new life.
Loose Women as they chat about news, issues and life in general.
Brian McFadden presents the dining challenge show. Superbike champion and King of the Jungle Carl Fogarty plays mystery dinner party host to four strangers.
Ekin-Su Culculoglu, Hugh Dennis, Kelly Hoppen and Shaun Wright-Phillips attempt to impress their loved ones and mentors to avoid the cook-off.

Two planes hit the Twin Towers in New York; the foot-and-mouth outbreak; Eamon Dunphy tries television presenting.
Alan Quinlan speaks about dealing with anxiety and other mental health strains during his career, which featured several highlight moments as well.
An activist, a barrister, a renowned pundit, Joe Brolly opens up in a rare and emotional interview about his life, his trauma, and his football controversies.

Four strangers answer trivia questions against quizzer who stands against them.
Presided over by outspoken Judge Judith Sheindlin; with her no-nonsense, decisive approach to justice; she has established herself as a pop-culture icon for generations of viewers.
